01 — Current State

The Process, Today

2–3h
Standard Takeoff
4–5h
Large Project
High
Error Exposure
📄
Full Manual Scanning
Complete review of frame schedules, door schedules, wall schedules, hardware sets, floor plans, elevations, sections, and details — every time.
🔁
Manual Cross-Referencing
Data must be manually reconciled between schedules and floor plans, with no automated link between sources.
🗂️
Manual Spreadsheet Entry
Every frame and door item entered by hand into the take-off spreadsheet. Time-consuming and highly repetitive.
⚠️
Fragile to Revision
Any change to the architecture set triggers a full re-review. Edits compound errors silently across the document.
02 / 08
AVALON · TAKE-OFF
02 — The Opportunity

Why This Matters

10%
Project Conversion Rate

Every 10 takeoffs produced yields, on average, one confirmed order.

The conversion rate isn't the bottleneck — volume is. More takeoffs in the pipeline means more orders won, without changing close rates.

If one estimator currently produces 2–3 takeoffs per day, reducing that cycle by 70% effectively multiplies output without adding headcount.

The goal is not to replace the estimator — it's to eliminate the mechanical overhead so they focus on decision-making, not data entry.

03 / 08
AVALON · TAKE-OFF
03 — Solution Framework

The Lever

Objective 01
Increase Takeoff Volume
Holding conversion rate constant, increasing the number of takeoffs produced directly scales revenue potential with no change in sales strategy.
Objective 02
Reduce Per-Takeoff Cycle Time
Target a 60–80% reduction in manual effort per takeoff through AI-assisted document parsing and structured output generation.
Objective 03
No Proportional Headcount Growth
The solution should scale capacity without requiring a linear increase in staff. Tooling absorbs repetitive workload; people handle review and judgment.
Same Team
×
AI Tooling
=
Higher Output
Conversion rate held constant at 10%. Variable is total takeoff volume per week. Each reduction in cycle time directly compounds — 2× faster = 2× opportunities in the pipeline.
04 / 08
AVALON · TAKE-OFF
04 — Evaluated Directions

Build vs. Buy

Option A — Third-Party Platform

Subscribe to an existing
takeoff platform

Evaluated: Fresco · Togal.AI · Autodesk Forma · Stack · Kreo.AI ★ Most Promising
✓Near-immediate implementation available
✓Kreo.AI: strong auto-detect, intuitive UI, Excel export with custom structure
✓Trial / workshop available with our own project files
✗~$150–$250/month per user. Expensive relative to output quality
✗No platform is aluminum-frame specific — all are universal tools
✗No error detection or conflict flagging
✗No native interoperability with Avalon's existing frame/door schedule
Option B — In-House AI Tool

Develop a proprietary
Avalon-native takeoff engine

Proof of concept completed internally
✓Cost of development < ongoing subscription cost for inferior tools
✓Purpose-built for Avalon — frame types, processes, schedule formats
✓Real-time iteration. Can adapt as processes evolve
✓Full interoperability with internal workflows and data formats
✓Positions Avalon as a digital-first operation — long-term asset
✗Longer build-to-deploy timeline vs plug-and-play
✗Gray areas and edge cases harder to predict upfront

05 — Proof of Concept

The Pipeline

🌐
Web-Based
Tool
→
📂
PDF Drop
Arch. Set
→
⚙️
Backend
Processing
→
🤖
AI Vision
Parser
→
📊
Web Viewer
Summary
→
⬇️
Excel
Download
The web viewer produces a structured summary of the entire architectural set — element types, quantities, scope notes, and conflict flags. This kicks off the takeoff process with all data already organized, reducing or eliminating the initial manual scan entirely.
Output 01
Complete door & frame schedule parsing and floor plan cross-referencing with type, quantity, dimensions, hardware sets, fire rating, and STC.
Output 02
Conflict detection layer — flags inconsistencies between floor plans and schedules automatically.
Output 03
Excel export pre-formatted to Avalon's take-off template. Ready for pricing, no manual re-entry.

06 — First Pass Results

What It Produced

Run against a live architectural set. All outputs generated automatically — no manual input after PDF upload.

Input — Architecture set PDF (door schedule, A62-01)
Output — Auto-generated Excel takeoff export
Web Viewer — AI takeoff summary with 29 openings parsed: frames, doors, borrowed lights, conflicts flagged
